description: Laboratory tests for Resident Life simulator - values, normal ranges, turnaround times, and costs

interpretation.<0.04: Normal - excludes MI if measured >3 hours after symptom onset
interpretation.0.04-1.0: Indeterminate - repeat in 1-3 hours. 0-hour/1-hour algorithm needed
interpretation.1.0-10.0: Elevated - consistent with myocardial injury. Consider NSTEMI
interpretation.>10.0: Markedly elevated - large myocardial infarction likely
interpretation.massive_pe: 1.0-5.0 - RV strain causing myocardial injury, not primary MI

interpretation.5-25: Borderline elevation - may be skeletal muscle source
interpretation.25-100: Elevated - consistent with MI. Check CK-MB index (CK-MB/CK >2.5%)
interpretation.>100: Markedly elevated - large MI or reperfusion

interpretation.100-500: Gray zone - possible HF if clinical suspicion high
interpretation.>5000: Severely elevated - acute decompensated heart failure likely
interpretation.copd: 100-400 - COPD alone rarely elevates BNP significantly
interpretation.renal: BNP can be elevated in renal failure even without HF

interpretation.wbc_high: Leukocytosis - infection, inflammation, stress, steroids, leukemia
interpretation.wbc_low: Leukopenia - viral infection, sepsis, bone marrow suppression, autoimmune
interpretation.hb_low: Anemia - acute blood loss, chronic disease, iron deficiency, hemolysis
interpretation.hb_high: Polycythemia - COPD, high altitude, polycythemia vera, dehydration
interpretation.plt_low: Thrombocytopenia - ITP, DIC, HIT, bone marrow failure, drugs, sepsis
interpretation.plt_high: Thrombocytosis - reactive (infection, inflammation, iron deficiency), myeloproliferative
interpretation.sepsis_typical: WBC 15-25 or <4, Hb normal or low if chronic, Plt 100-150 (early) or <50 (DIC)
interpretation.mi_typical: WBC 10-15 (stress response), Hb normal, Plt normal

interpretation.inr_high: Prolonged PT/INR - warfarin effect, liver disease, vitamin K deficiency, DIC
interpretation.ptt_high: Prolonged PTT - heparin effect, lupus anticoagulant, factor deficiency
interpretation.both_high: Prolonged PT and PTT - DIC, severe liver disease, massive transfusion
interpretation.dic: PT>15, PTT>45, INR>1.5, Plt<100, D-dimer elevated, Fibrinogen low

interpretation.k_high: >5.0 Hyperkalemia - renal failure, medications (ACEi/ARB/spironolactone), tissue breakdown, acidosis, pseudohyperkalemia
interpretation.k_low: <3.5 Hypokalemia - diuretics, vomiting/diarrhea, insulin, alkalosis, Cushing's
interpretation.k_critical_high: >6.5 - CARDIAC ARREST RISK - immediate ECG and treatment (calcium gluconate, insulin+glucose, bicarb, dialysis)
interpretation.na_high: >145 Hypernatremia - dehydration, diabetes insipidus, hypertonic fluids
interpretation.na_low: <135 Hyponatremia - SIADH, diuretics, heart failure, cirrhosis, polydipsia
interpretation.na_critical: <120 - Seizure risk - slow correction needed (max 8-10 mmol/day)

interpretation.aki_stage1: Cr increase 1.5-1.9x baseline OR >0.3 mg/dL increase
interpretation.aki_stage3: Cr increase 3.0x baseline OR Cr >4.0 OR on RRT
interpretation.bun_cr_ratio: >20:1 suggests pre-renal AKI (dehydration, HF). <10:1 suggests intrinsic renal disease.

chemistry.name: Liver Function Tests (ALT, AST, ALP, Bilirubin, Albumin)

interpretation.hepatocellular: ALT/AST >1000 - viral hepatitis, acetaminophen toxicity, ischemic hepatitis
interpretation.cholestatic: ALP >300, GGT elevated - biliary obstruction, cholangitis
interpretation.cirrhosis: Low albumin, prolonged PT, normal/mild ALT/AST elevation, low platelets

interpretation.severe_hypoglycemia: <50 mg/dL - neuroglycopenic symptoms, D50 or glucagon
interpretation.dka: 250-800 mg/dL - associated with anion gap acidosis, ketones positive
interpretation.hss: >600 mg/dL - hyperosmolar state, severe dehydration, no ketones

interpretation.5-40: Mildly elevated - viral infection, mild inflammation
interpretation.40-200: Moderately elevated - bacterial infection, active inflammation, pericarditis
interpretation.>200: Severely elevated - severe bacterial infection, sepsis, active autoimmune disease

interpretation.0.05-0.5: Mild elevation - possible localized infection, viral with bacterial superinfection
interpretation.0.5-2.0: Moderate elevation - bacterial infection likely, start antibiotics
interpretation.>2.0: High - severe bacterial sepsis, continue antibiotics
interpretation.>10.0: Very high - severe septic shock, high mortality risk

interpretation.2.0-4.0: Mildly elevated - monitor, may indicate early hypoperfusion
interpretation.>4.0: Significantly elevated - tissue hypoperfusion, sepsis, shock. MORTALITY RISK
interpretation.sepsis: Lactate >2.0 with infection = Sepsis (Sepsis-3). Lactate >4.0 = septic shock if hypotensive.
interpretation.metformin: Lactate >5.0 in patient on metformin - consider metformin-associated lactic acidosis (MALA)
interpretation.ischemic_bowel: Lactate >4.0 + abdominal pain + acidosis = mesenteric ischemia until proven otherwise

interpretation.respiratory_acidosis: pH <7.35, pCO2 >45 - COPD, respiratory depression, neuromuscular weakness
interpretation.respiratory_alkalosis: pH >7.45, pCO2 <35 - hyperventilation, anxiety, pain, early sepsis
interpretation.metabolic_acidosis: pH <7.35, HCO3 <22 - check anion gap (Na - Cl - HCO3)
interpretation.high_anion_gap: >12 - MUDPILES: Methanol, Uremia, DKA, Paraldehyde, Iron/INH, Lactic acidosis, Ethylene glycol, Salicylates
interpretation.normal_anion_gap: <12 - HARDUPS: Hyperalimentation, Acetazolamide, RTA, Diarrhea, Ureteral diversion, Pancreatic fistula, Saline
interpretation.metabolic_alkalosis: pH >7.45, HCO3 >26 - vomiting, diuretics, volume contraction, hypokalemia

cardiac_enzymes_trend.description: Expected troponin trends for different conditions
cardiac_enzymes_trend.stemi: T0: 0.1-0.5, T1h: 5-15, T3h: 20-50+ (rapid rise and peak)
